formulas.name: Work Done by a Constant Force
formulas.notes: Work is done only when the force and displacement are in the same direction.
formulas.variables: W = Work done, F = Force applied, s = Displacement, θ = Angle between force and displacement

formulas.name: Work Done by a Variable Force
formulas.notes: This formula is used when the force is variable.
formulas.variables: W = Work done, F = Force applied, ds = Small displacement

formulas.name: Kinetic Energy of an Object
formulas.notes: Kinetic energy is the energy of motion.
formulas.variables: KE = Kinetic energy, m = Mass of the object, v = Velocity of the object

formulas.name: Potential Energy of an Object at Height
formulas.notes: Potential energy is the energy of position.
formulas.variables: PE = Potential energy, m = Mass of the object, g = Acceleration due to gravity, h = Height of the object

formulas.name: Potential Energy of an Object at a Depth
formulas.notes: This formula is used for an object attached to a spring.
formulas.variables: PE = Potential energy, k = Spring constant, x = Displacement from equilibrium position

formulas.name: Potential Energy of an Object due to Gravitational Potential Energy
formulas.notes: This formula is used when the object is at a certain height.
formulas.variables: PE = Potential energy, m = Mass of the object, g = Acceleration due to gravity, h = Height of the object

formulas.name: Potential Energy of an Object due to Elastic Potential Energy
formulas.notes: This formula is used for an object attached to a spring.
formulas.variables: PE = Potential energy, k = Spring constant, x = Displacement from equilibrium position

formulas.name: Power
formulas.notes: Power is the rate of doing work.
formulas.variables: P = Power, W = Work done, t = Time taken

formulas.name: Work-Energy Theorem
formulas.notes: This theorem states that the change in kinetic energy of an object is equal to the change in its potential energy plus the work done on it.
formulas.variables: ΔK = Change in kinetic energy, ΔU = Change in potential energy, W = Work done

formulas.name: Law of Conservation of Energy
formulas.notes: This law states that the total energy of an isolated system remains constant.
formulas.variables: E = Total energy, KE = Kinetic energy, PE = Potential energy
